recommendations.name: MX-5 ND Axle-Back Exhaust (Borla S-Type)
recommendations.description: Borla's S-Type axle-back exhaust for the ND MX-5 delivers a throaty, sporty exhaust note without drone at highway speeds. Replaces only the rear muffler section, making it one of the easiest bolt-on sound upgrades available. Ideal for a daily driver who wants noticeable but not obnoxious sound.
brand_tips: Racing Beat Exhaust for ND Miata

recommendations.name: Cold Air / Short Ram Intake (K&N 69-Series)
recommendations.description: K&N's 69-Series Typhoon short ram intake replaces the stock airbox on the 2017 MX-5. Beyond a small performance boost, it adds a satisfying induction growl and whoosh sound under acceleration that complements an exhaust upgrade nicely. Easy drop-in installation with no tuning required.
brand_tips: Mishimoto Performance Intake for ND MX-5

recommendations.name: Resonator Delete (OEM Resonator Bypass Pipe)
recommendations.description: Replacing the mid-pipe resonator with a straight-through or high-flow bypass pipe frees up exhaust tone in the mid-range frequencies, giving the car a more characterful sound throughout the RPM range. Best paired with an axle-back for a fuller, richer note. Clamps directly onto stock mid-pipe.
brand_tips: DIY custom pipe from exhaust shop ($40–60)

recommendations.name: Plasti-Dip Engine Bay Sound Dampening (Firewall)
recommendations.description: Applying Dynamat or Noico self-adhesive sound-deadening mat to the firewall and transmission tunnel reduces road and mechanical noise intrusion into the cabin, making the exhaust and intake sounds you've added the dominant audio experience — a great complement to the other mods.
brand_tips: Noico 80 mil Sound Deadener (50 sq ft roll)

recommendations.name: Chassis Brace / Strut Tower Brace (for better acoustic feedback)
recommendations.description: A front strut tower brace on the ND MX-5 tightens up chassis flex, which subtly improves the mechanical 'feel and sound' feedback through the car — steering inputs and engine sounds translate more crisply. Brands like Mazdaspeed and Mishimoto make direct bolt-on fitments.

recommendations.name: Sport Horn Upgrade
recommendations.description: Swap the weak stock Miata horn for a louder, higher-quality dual-tone Hella or Fiamm sport horn. A fun, easy upgrade that takes under 30 minutes and makes the car feel more complete. Plug-and-play with the stock wiring harness.

recommendations.name: Intake Snorkel / Velocity Stack Sound Enhancer
recommendations.description: An aftermarket velocity stack or intake snorkel extension fitted to the airbox or K&N intake amplifies the induction roar inside the cabin on the 2017 MX-5. Some ND owners pair these with an opened-up airbox lid for a louder engine note with zero risk.
brand_tips: Beatrush Airbox Sound Enhancer for ND
DIY snorkel mod via Miata.net forum guides
